summary: Matched seven GR stage modules by replacing their assembly files with C sources and headers: grfigure1, grfigure2, grfigure3, grtdonkey, grtfalcon, grtpichu, and grtyoshi. The new C implementations encode StageData and StageCallbacks tables, stage init callbacks, GObj creation/setup, map callback assignment, and small stub/return functions. obj_files.mk was updated to build the new C objects instead of the old asm objects. grtkoopa.c was lightly cleaned by removing temporary '// header' comments from static functions.
what_changed: Deleted the assembly implementations for grfigure1, grfigure2, grfigure3, grtdonkey, grtfalcon, grtpichu, and grtyoshi under asm/melee/gr.
Added matching C source files for those seven GR modules under src/melee/gr.
Added per-module headers declaring the static functions used by each new C file and including global.h, melee/gr/stage.h, and sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h.
Changed obj_files.mk entries from asm/melee/gr/*.s.o to src/melee/gr/*.c.o for the seven matched modules.
Represented module data with static StageCallbacks arrays and extern StageData records, preserving asset paths such as /GrEF1.dat, /GrEF2.dat, /GrTCa.dat, and /GrTPc.dat.

Implemented repeated stage setup flow: set stage_info.unk8C.b4 to 0 and b5 to 1, create required map GObjs by ID, then call common stage functions such as func_801C39C0 and func_801C3BB4; trophy-stage modules also call func_801C4210 and func_801C42AC.

Implemented common GObj setup helpers that call func_801C14D0, clear Map x8_callback and xC_callback, call GObj_SetupGXLink with func_801C5DB0, install callback3 into map->x1C_callback when present, invoke callback0, and register callback2 with func_8038FD54.
Kept OSReport failure strings for missing GObjs, including source-file names and line-number constants needed for matching.
Cleaned grtkoopa.c by removing temporary '// header' comments from existing static function definitions without changing the apparent function bodies.
smart_moves: Matched several highly repetitive GR modules in one PR by reusing the same StageCallbacks/StageData and GObj setup pattern across figure and trophy-stage files.
Preserved assembly-era symbol names such as lbl_803E6278, lbl_803E62C0, func_8020E080, and lbl_804D6A70 where semantic names were not yet established, reducing matching risk.
Used explicit StageCallbacks arrays indexed by gobj_id to mirror the assembly table layout and make callback dispatch straightforward.
Used Map* map = gobj->user_data and named fields like x8_callback, xC_callback, x1C_callback, hsd_obj, and map_id, which made raw offset behavior readable while still matching.
Inserted local unused stack arrays such as u32 unused[2] in callback functions where needed to influence stack layout for matching.
Kept small empty callbacks and constant-return callbacks as separate static functions rather than folding them away, preserving function boundaries and table entries.
Updated build wiring in obj_files.mk in the same change as the asm deletion, making the asm-to-C replacement complete.
decomp_lessons: For GR stage files, first model the data tables: StageCallbacks arrays and StageData records often reveal the function roles and object ordering.
Repeated stage modules can often be matched from a template: init stage_info flags, spawn GObjs by ID, set common GX link, install map callbacks, and provide no-op/constant callbacks.
String literals in OSReport calls, including file names and line-number constants, are part of the match and should be preserved carefully.
When matching table-driven modules, keep callback function prototypes consistent with how the table calls them, but also watch project-wide primitive type conventions.
Small unused locals can be an acceptable matching tactic when stack frame shape requires them.
Moving an object from asm to C requires both deleting the old asm file and changing obj_files.mk to point at the new C object.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions and data kept address-derived names: func_8020E080, lbl_8020DFE0, lbl_803E6278, lbl_804D6A70, etc.
New headers used lowercase include guards based on file names, e.g. _grfigure1_h_ and _grtyoshi_h_.
Stage data records and callback arrays retained lbl_803E**** names instead of speculative semantic names.
Common map/stage types used project types such as StageCallbacks, StageData, StageInfo, HSD_GObj, Map, Vec, and HSD_JObj.
Reviewer guidance: prefer fixed-size/project primitive types such as BOOL and s32 over plain int and long unless there is a specific reason.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly files wholesale with C implementations and switched obj_files.mk to compile the C objects.
Matched rodata/data layout with static StageCallbacks arrays followed by extern StageData initializers.
Used 0UL, NULL, and exact numeric constants such as 0xC0000000, stage IDs, and StageData trailing flags to mirror original table contents.
Preserved exact GObj setup call order: func_801C14D0, clear callbacks, GObj_SetupGXLink, optional callback3 assignment, optional callback0 call, optional func_8038FD54 registration.
Preserved stage_info bitfield writes as stage_info.unk8C.b4 = 0 and stage_info.unk8C.b5 = 1, corresponding to the original rlwimi byte manipulation.
Used empty static functions for assembly blr-only routines and simple return 0 / return 1 functions for constant callbacks.
Added unused local arrays in some callbacks to shape generated code/stack behavior.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.comment: Please use fixed-size primitive types in the future unless there is a specific reason. The reviewer suggested static BOOL lbl_80223E1C(s32); instead of static int lbl_80223E1C(long); and noted it did not affect the match for this PR.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803E**** StageData or StageCallbacks symbols in melee/gr.
Search for follow-up changes replacing int/long prototypes with BOOL/s32 in grtyoshi or related GR headers.
Compare grtkoopa.c against grtfalcon, grtpichu, grtdonkey, and grtyoshi for reusable trophy-stage matching templates.
Search for other GR modules still built from asm/melee/gr in obj_files.mk after this PR.
Look for later cleanup of header include guards and static prototypes in src/melee/gr/*.h.
